task: I’m comparing a couple of used car listings and one seller is pushing a fast deposit before I can see the full paperwork. Can you help me make a quick checklist of what I should verify first so I don’t miss anything important?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — if they’re pushing a fast deposit, verify the important stuff **before** sending anything.

## Quick used car checklist

### 1) Seller and title
- [ ] Seller name matches title/registration
- [ ] Title is in hand
- [ ] No lien unless they can prove payoff process
- [ ] VIN on title matches VIN on car

### 2) Car identity
- [ ] VIN matches listing
- [ ] Mileage matches listing
- [ ] Trim / options match what was advertised
- [ ] No obvious signs of accident or salvage history

### 3) Paperwork
- [ ] Service records available
- [ ] Bill of sale terms are clear
- [ ] Any fees are disclosed
- [ ] Emissions / inspection status is known
- [ ] Warranty terms are clear if included

### 4) Condition
- [ ] Exterior / interior condition checked
- [ ] Tires / brakes / lights inspected
- [ ] No warning lights on dash
- [ ] Test drive completed
- [ ] Mechanic inspection if possible

### 5) Payment / deposit
- [ ] Deposit amount is reasonable
- [ ] Deposit terms are in writing
- [ ] Refundability is clear
- [ ] No pressure to pay before seeing paperwork
- [ ] Payment method is safe and traceable

## Red flags
- rushing for a deposit
- won’t show title or paperwork
- VIN doesn’t match
- story changes
- price is far below market
- won’t allow inspection

## Safe rule
**No deposit until the title, VIN, seller identity, and basic paperwork are verified.**

If you want, I can turn this into a **short red-flag checklist** you can use on the phone while talking to the seller.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is close. I’d want it a little tighter and more specific before I use it on a call.

Put the top 5 in order of priority, like:
- seller name + ID matches title
- VIN on title matches car
- title is clean / lien status explained
- exact out-the-door price and deposit terms in writing
- mechanic inspection or at least permission to inspect

Also add one line for the big scam stuff: no deposit if they won’t share photos of the title, VIN plate, and current registration first.

I’d use that before anything else. If you want, make it into a one-screen text version I can keep open while I’m messaging them.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s the tighter version.

## Top 5 checks, in order
1. **Seller name + ID matches the title**
2. **VIN on the title matches the car**
3. **Title status is clear**  
   - clean title, or  
   - lien explained with payoff process in writing
4. **Exact out-the-door price + deposit terms are in writing**
5. **Mechanic inspection is allowed**  
   - or at least you get permission to inspect before paying

## Big scam rule
**No deposit if they won’t send photos of:**
- the title
- the VIN plate
- current registration

## Quick safe rule
If any of the top 5 are missing or vague, **don’t send money yet**.

If you want, I can make this into an even shorter **phone-call / text-message version**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and the order makes sense.

One-screen version would be useful too — the kind I can keep open while texting them.
